steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim states that Aristophanes satirizes sophists as having an exaggerated sense of their own wisdom. The correct quotation must show a sophist boasting about his own cleverness — not someone else praising sophists, and not a general description of their school.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Strepsiades praises sophists': This shows an outsider admiring sophists, not a sophist puffing up his own wisdom. ✗
  • Choice B — 'describes the school in general': A general description of the think-shop doesn't show a sophist's inflated self-regard. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Socrates on "clever dogma about abstruse matters"': Socrates, cast as a sophist, self-importantly describes his own teachings as clever and dealing with abstruse matters — a direct display of exaggerated self-wisdom. ✓
  • Choice D — 'criticizes a customer': Disparaging a student doesn't demonstrate the sophist's inflated view of his own wisdom. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
